name: UNIFIL — UN Interim Force in Lebanon
detail: Established by UNSCR 425 (1978) after Israel's Operation Litani. Expanded to 15,000 by UNSCR 1701 (2006). Currently ~10,500 troops from 50+ nations. HQ at Naqoura, southern Lebanon. Mission: monitor Blue Line, support Lebanese Armed Forces, prevent hostile acts. Repeatedly obstructed by IDF in 2024; several UNIFIL positions hit by Israeli fire.

name: Lebanese Armed Forces (LAF)
detail: Founded 1945; approximately 80,000 active personnel. Constitutionally neutral institution respected across sects. Deliberately underfunded by political class to prevent it challenging militias. US is primary donor ($3B+ since 2006). Post-2024 ceasefire: deploying south of Litani for first time since 1969 under UNSCR 1701 framework. Commanded by now-President Joseph Aoun until Jan 2025.

name: Iran IRGC-Quds Force — Hezbollah Patron
detail: Iranian Revolutionary Guards Corps-Quds Force has funded, trained, and armed Hezbollah since 1982. Annual Iranian support estimated at $700M–$1B pre-2024. After 2024 conflict Hezbollah's military infrastructure degraded; Iran committed to rebuilding. Commander Esmail Qaani coordinates with Hezbollah. Iran views Hezbollah as its most important regional proxy and strategic deterrence against Israel.

name: Hezbollah Islamic Resistance
detail: Estimated 30,000–50,000 fighters (regular + reserves) pre-2024; significant degradation after 2024 conflict. Arsenal pre-2024: 130,000+ rockets and missiles including precision-guided munitions, anti-ship missiles, drones. Organisationally devastated in 2024: Nasrallah killed Sep 27; Unit 900 (pager) destroyed Sep 17; multiple regional commanders eliminated. New SG Naim Qassem appointed. Retreated north of Litani under ceasefire.

name: France — Historic Protector and Diplomatic Actor
detail: France has maintained a special relationship with Lebanon since the Crusades and Maronite protection treaties (1535 Franco-Ottoman accord). Troops deployed 1860, 1920 Mandate, 1982–1984 MNF, 2006 UNIFIL. Co-brokered Nov 2024 ceasefire. Macron flew to Beirut August 2020 (explosion) and October 2024. Hosts Lebanon donor conferences. Lebanon remains a Francophone country; 40% French-proficiency rate.

name: United States — Peacekeepers, Aid Donors, Ceasefire Brokers
detail: US Marines landed in Lebanon in 1958 (Operation Blue Bat) and 1982–1984 (MNF). Barracks bombing killed 241 Americans Oct 1983. US has provided $3B+ military aid to Lebanese Armed Forces since 2006. Led ceasefire negotiations in Nov 2024. US ambassadors actively involved in Lebanese politics; sanctions on Hezbollah affect Lebanese banking. Biden administration brokered Joseph Aoun's election.

name: South Lebanon Army (SLA) 1979–2000
detail: Israeli-backed Lebanese militia created after Operation Litani, led by Major Sa'd Haddad then General Antoine Lahad. Administered Israel's Security Zone in South Lebanon 1985–2000. Manned Khiam detention centre (torture documented by Amnesty International). Collapsed when Israel withdrew May 2000; ~7,000 SLA members and families fled to Israel. Represents Lebanon's most significant Israel-collaboration episode.

name: Saudi Arabia / Gulf States — Economic Leverage
detail: Saudi Arabia and Gulf states host ~400,000 Lebanese workers whose remittances sustain Lebanon ($7B/year). Saudi expelled Lebanese ambassador 2021 over Hezbollah minister's statements; partial re-engagement 2022–2023. Gulf states conditionally supportive of Aoun-Salam government. Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia are key potential reconstruction donors. Lebanon's relations with Gulf historically mediated by Hariri family and Sunni community ties.

name: IMF / World Bank — Creditors and Reform Monitors
detail: Lebanon reached a staff-level agreement with the IMF in April 2022 ($3B package) but parliament failed to pass enabling legislation. World Bank provides non-political grants for humanitarian projects and analytical reports. IMF estimates Lebanon's financial sector losses at $72B. World Bank estimates 2024 war reconstruction needs at $8.5B. Both institutions conditioning support on banking sector reform, BdL restructuring, and anti-corruption measures.

name: Special Tribunal for Lebanon (STL)
detail: Established 2007 by UNSCR 1757; hybrid UN-Lebanese tribunal investigating the Feb 14, 2005 Hariri assassination. In August 2020, convicted Hezbollah member Salim Ayyash in absentia for conspiracy to commit terrorist act. Three other accused acquitted for lack of evidence. Hezbollah refused to surrender Ayyash. STL officially closed in September 2023. The Hariri case officially remains with unserved sentences.
